response:
## What `src/billing.ts` does

A 310-line billing module centred on `generateInvoice` (lines 50–253, 204 lines) plus a `calculateRefundPreview` helper that — despite its read-only name — calls `saveRefundAudit` and `sendRefundEmail` (lines 40–44). Five top-level functions total. The file reads wall-clock time directly (7× `Date.now()`/`new Date()` between lines 57 and 273) and carries an enterprise-plan refund rule encoded only in a comment at line 39: *"Only owners can refund enterprise plans unless billing support approves."*

## Files I'd also want to read before editing

- **`src/billing.test.ts`** — the existing tests are weak (one has zero assertions, another uses only a weak matcher), so don't trust them as a safety net.
- **`src/date.ts`** — already has its own `Date.now()`/`new Date()` problem; likely where a shared clock abstraction should live.
- **`src/api/plan.ts`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts`, `src/ui/pricing.ts`** — all repeat the `"enterprise"` literal alongside billing.ts; refund/plan logic is probably duplicated across them.
- **`src/nav/registry.ts`, `src/nav/sidebar.ts`, `src/routes/settings/billing.tsx`** — the `/settings/billing` destination is labelled inconsistently (`Plans` vs `Billing` vs `Subscription` vs component `PricingPage`); relevant if billing changes touch the UI surface.
- **`docs/billing.md`** — flagged as part of the `tenant`/`account`/`organisation`/`team`/`workspace` alias drift; check the canonical vocabulary before naming anything new.

## Risks the scan flagged for this file

| Severity | Charge | Why it matters here |
|---|---|---|
| **high** | God Function (`generateInvoice`, 204 lines, 3.4× threshold) | Edits to one section easily miss interactions elsewhere |
| medium | Temporal Recklessness (7 direct `Date` calls) | Non-deterministic; tests can't pin a moment |
| medium | False Identity (`calculateRefundPreview` does side effects) | Callers may treat it as pure |
| medium | God File (310 lines, just over threshold) | Hidden local coupling |
| medium | Logic in Comments (refund-approval rule only in prose) | Rule isn't enforced in code |
| medium | String Sprinkles (`"enterprise"` in 4 files) | Policy likely duplicated |
| low | Unfinished Business (4 TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX markers, lines 183–308) | Hard to tell which logic is load-bearing |

The aggregate signal: this is a load-bearing file with a giant central function, hidden temporal coupling, a misleadingly-named side-effect helper, and a refund-policy rule that lives only in a comment. Before changing behaviour, read the test file to know how little protection exists, and check the three other files sharing the `"enterprise"` literal so any refund/plan rule change happens in one place rather than four.
